last_completed_round.direction_name: Shift The Hot-Band / Peeled Seam Down By One PTX Block
last_completed_round.hypothesis: Round 13/17 closed the 64x384 control decisively: runtime exploded to 33.9645443 ms, DRAM hit 55.24, and barrier reached 17.01, so the broad fixed-main-tile branch is not competitive. That leaves the seam between the accepted PTX hot-band kernel and the peeled 384-row band as the cleanest remaining bounded lever. The next step should shift `kFixedPivotHotRows` down by one 128-row PTX block so the peeled path absorbs more of the bottom rows, without changing the PTX kernel internals themselves.

notes: Build passed for round 15/17. Node A will measure the result next.
